body.title: [sprint] Implement the ONE escalation dispatch rule: failure routes to producer review, not admin
body.description:
intention: I intend that spec-shaped failures land in the producer's review queue with their produced_by chain attached, and zero land in the generic admin bucket.

Per T-feedback-edge-design: implement the single dispatch rule. When a worker/station emits 'couldn't solve' (or any downstream failure on an artifact), resolve the artifact's produced_by chain and route the escalation to the producer's review (producing decision goes to review/open_questions), NOT to the generic admin/escalated bucket. Tier escalation (capability miss) stays untouched and runs first; produced_by dispatch fires for spec-shaped failures or top-tier exhaustion. All ticket-state mutations go through Granny's write-path (D-granny-sole-ticket-writer-2026-07-07). Cycle guard: dispatch refuses and logs WARN if the produced_by chain revisits an artifact.

**Affected files:** per design artifact — expected: unseen_university/devices/granny/daemon.py escalation path, devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py escalate path. If the design requires a bus-envelope change (unseen_university/devices/bus/ = HIGH-inertia): STOP — Akien pre-approval required BEFORE edit; stamp approval here.

**Design rules:** log every state change + interface crossing (AR-009); Granny sole ticket writer; no per-artifact-type escalation paths.

**Scope boundary:** dispatch rule + log lines only; no changes to tier-escalation ladder; no UI.

**Test plan:** hermetic test — artifact with produced_by chain, fire failure, assert escalation record targets producer and admin bucket stays empty; cycle-guard test. Cleanup: tmp store root + mocked Granny write-path; fixture teardown.

**Completion criteria:** test suite green including cycle guard; first real escalation post-ship logs route=producer-review; admin-bucket escalations from produced_by-carrying artifacts = 0.

**Validation:** Observable via: log.info("escalation: artifact=<id> -> producer=<id> route=producer-review") at the dispatch point; log.warning on cycle guard.

**Rollback:** git revert restores prior admin-queue routing — rule is a routing layer, not a data migration.

audit-emphasis: needs-deep-smell

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
